commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: Mark, who studies brevity more than any of the Evangelists, has given a concise indication of Pilate’s desire and of his efforts to save the Lord’s life. For, after giving us the statement, “And they cried again, ‘Crucify him’” (which makes it clear that they had cried out before for Barabbas to be released), he has appended these words: “Then Pilate continued to say to them, “Why, what evil has he done?” Thus by one short sentence he has given us an idea of matters which took a long time for their transaction. .

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
